title: Bidens dissecta
intro: Bidens dissecta, also known as dissected beggarticks, is a perennial herb belonging to the Asteraceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its analgesic, antipyretic, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Bidens dissecta include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Bidens dissecta include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to relieve coughs, colds, and other respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Bidens dissecta is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the family Asteraceae. This family is commonly known as the sunflower or aster family. Bidens dissecta is further classified as a member of the genus Bidens, which consists of about 200 species of plants. The genus Bidens is a part of the tribe Bidenteae, which is one of the largest and most diverse tribes within the Asteraceae family. Further classification may include the order Asterales, which is a group of flowering plants that also includes sunflowers and daisies.
